title: Epigram. Dialogue Between A Dowager And Her Maid On The Night Of Lord Yarmouth's Fete.
content:
"I want the Court Guide," said my lady, "to look
        "If the House, Seymour Place, be at 30. or 20."--
    "We've lost the Court Guide, Ma'am, but here's the Red Book.
        "Where you'll find, I dare say, Seymour Places in plenty!"
meaning:
Exploring the themes of classic, Thomas Moore delivers a powerful performance in "Epigram. Dialogue Between A Dowager And Her Maid On The Night Of Lord Yarmouth's Fete."...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
